questions.question: What is the difference between erosion and dilation? (2 marks)
expectedPoints: Erosion (⊖) computes the local minimum, shrinking objects and removing small details.
Dilation (⊕) computes the local maximum, expanding objects and filling holes/gaps.

questions.question: Explain the purpose and process of the morphological closing operation. Use an example to illustrate your answer. (5 marks)
expectedPoints: Purpose: Closing (A • B) is used to fill small holes and gaps within foreground objects and to connect nearby objects.
Process: It is defined as dilation followed by erosion: A • B = (A ⊕ B) ⊖ B.
Dilation Step: The initial dilation expands the foreground regions, filling in small holes and breaking.
Erosion Step: The subsequent erosion shrinks the enlarged objects back towards their original size, but the filled holes remain as they are now part of the foreground.
Example: Used to fill a small hole inside a character 'O' in an image of text, making it a solid blob.
